title: AI Can Launch Cyberattacks for Just $1 — Here's How We Fight Back
summary: Imagine a robot that can break into any computer for less than the price of a candy bar. That's almost real today! New AI programs, like Claude Mythos, can find weak spots in software super fast — sometimes in minutes — and it costs just pocket change. But here's the twist: the same AI can also help protect us. It has already found over a thousand hidden bugs in things like web browsers and operating systems, so companies can fix them before bad guys attack. This is like a race. In the past, finding a software bug took months. Now AI does it in a flash. The problem is, fixing those bugs still needs human engineers — and a lot of code, especially in free open-source projects, is maintained by tiny teams or even just one person in their spare time. Remember the Log4j bug in 2021? It was in a library used by millions of devices, but only a few volunteers took care of it. When a flaw was found, it caused a worldwide panic. Some people think we should just stop AI from being used for attacks, but that's tricky. Clever hackers can trick the AI by wording their requests carefully. And even if one country bans it, AI tools are available everywhere. Another idea is to let AI automatically fix bugs, but that's risky — what if the fix breaks something else? The best plan is to build software that's safer from the start, using something called "memory-safe" code. That's like using unbreakable bricks to build a house, so even if a burglar tries to get in, they can't. The future of cybersecurity is a high-tech game of cat and mouse, but with smarter building, we can stay ahead.
why_it_matters: The code that protects your online games, school accounts, and family photos relies on these behind‑the‑scenes bug hunts, so understanding the race helps you know why strong passwords and updates keep your digital life safe.

keywords.explanation: A new AI program that can quickly find weaknesses in computer software.
The main software that runs a whole computer, like Windows, macOS, or Linux.
A type of software that anyone can look at, use, and change for free.
A piece of code used by millions of devices that had a serious security bug in 2021.
A way of writing code that automatically stops common mistakes hackers use to break in.
A mistake or weak spot in software that can let attackers get inside a system.

questions.question: What can the AI program Claude Mythos do?
options: Cook dinner
Find weak spots in software quickly
Drive a car
Write a book
questions.correct_answer: Find weak spots in software quickly

questions.question: What problem did the Log4j bug cause?
options: It made computers run faster
It caused a worldwide panic because it was in millions of devices
It only affected one website
It deleted all photos from phones
questions.correct_answer: It caused a worldwide panic because it was in millions of devices

questions.question: Why is it hard to just ban AI from being used for attacks?
options: Because AI is too heavy to lift
Because hackers can trick AI with carefully worded requests and AI tools are available everywhere
Because only one country uses AI
Because AI doesn't exist
questions.correct_answer: Because hackers can trick AI with carefully worded requests and AI tools are available everywhere

questions.question: What is a big challenge for open-source software projects?
options: They cost too much money
They are often maintained by tiny teams or just one person
They can only run on old computers
They are illegal in most countries
questions.correct_answer: They are often maintained by tiny teams or just one person

questions.question: What does "memory-safe" code promise?
options: It remembers your passwords
It acts like unbreakable bricks, stopping many common hacking tricks
It makes software look prettier
It cooks food safely
questions.correct_answer: It acts like unbreakable bricks, stopping many common hacking tricks

questions.question: Why does fixing software bugs still need human engineers?
options: Because robots are on vacation
Because an automatic fix might break something else, and humans must check the code
Because only humans can use keyboards
Because laws say only humans can fix computers
questions.correct_answer: Because an automatic fix might break something else, and humans must check the code

background_read: Claude Mythos is an advanced AI tool built to understand and work with computer code, much like a super-fast digital assistant for programmers.
The Log4j flaw was discovered in December 2021 in a tiny piece of code used by countless websites, games, and cloud services, causing a rush to patch systems worldwide.
Open-source software is free for anyone to use, study, and improve, but many critical open-source projects rely on just a handful of unpaid volunteer developers.
Memory-safe programming languages, like Rust, are designed to prevent entire classes of bugs that let attackers take control of computers, making software safer from the start.
Article_Structure: WHO: AI programs like Claude Mythos, hackers, human engineers, and open-source volunteers.
WHAT: AI can now find software flaws in minutes for pocket change, creating both a defense tool and a risk for cyberattacks.
WHERE: In the global world of software, affecting web browsers, operating systems, and any connected device.
WHY: To explain why fixing bugs still takes human effort, why banning AI is hard, and how building with "memory-safe" code can be a long-term solution.

perspectives.description: AI can scan millions of lines of code almost instantly, helping companies fix holes before bad actors find them and making the internet safer for everyone.
Hackers can use the same AI to launch cheap, fast attacks, and small open-source teams often cannot respond quickly enough, putting everyone at risk.
The core problem is not just AI but the decades-old habit of writing software in languages that allow memory mistakes; switching to memory-safe code is a long, expensive process.
